recent_decisions.title: Minutes of previous meeting approved
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes of the previous meeting held on 29 September 2025.

motions.text: That the minutes of meeting Monday, 29 September 2025 of Community and External Scrutiny Committee be approved as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Recommendations tracking document updated
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted progress updates and agreed to remove completed recommendations from the tracking document.

motions.text: The Committee noted progress updates outlined within the report and agreed to remove completed recommendations from the tracking document.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from November 2025 meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the November 2025 meeting were unanimously approved as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Note asbestos management report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the report on asbestos management, acknowledging potential unidentified asbestos in council assets.

recent_decisions.title: Note internal audit progress report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the internal audit progress report, including concerns over contract management and volunteer arrangements.

recent_decisions.title: Defer repairs and maintenance review
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the deferral of the repairs and maintenance review for the reasons provided.

recent_decisions.title: Share volunteering policy with community hubs
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee agreed to share the outcome of the volunteering policy and procedures with community hubs.
key_data_points: Policy to be shared by June 2026
Led by Director of Change and Delivery

recent_decisions.title: Provide details on closed graveyards
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee requested details of the 4 outstanding management actions related to closed graveyards.

recent_decisions.title: Note annual governance statement update
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the update on the progress of the Annual Governance Statement management actions.

recent_decisions.title: Note audit committee effectiveness report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the report on the council's compliance with CIPFA guidance and the self-assessment of good practice.

recent_decisions.title: Note update on leisure governance
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the update on the governance processes of South Ribble Leisure Limited, with improvements expected by May 2026.
key_data_points: Report to be submitted in May

recent_decisions.title: Approve updated RIPA policy
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the updated policy for the use of covert surveillance and human intelligence sources, subject to a gender-neutrality amendment.

recent_decisions.title: Exclude press and public for confidential item
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously resolved to exclude the press and public for a confidential item involving exempt information.

recent_decisions.title: Note internal audit review of security incident response
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the confidential internal audit review of security incident detection and response.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of revised capital programme with £1.098m amendments
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously approved a revised capital programme with £1.098m changes, including £150k for Worden Park overflow car park resurfacing. The car park will add 200 spaces and generate £100k annually.
key_data_points: £150k allocated for Worden Park overflow car park
£100k annual income expected from car park

recent_decisions.title: Approval of liquid fuels contract with Standard Fuel Oils
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously approved a liquid fuels supply contract with Standard Fuel Oils, rejecting a switch to HVO fuel due to higher costs and environmental uncertainties.
key_data_points: Contract awarded to Standard Fuel Oils
HVO fuel option rejected due to cost and environmental concerns
Current contract ensures compliance with procurement regulations

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Q3 performance monitoring report
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Q3 performance report, accepting CPBS recommendations. Highlights included completion of Jubilee Gardens and increased customer satisfaction.
key_data_points: Jubilee Gardens residency on target for summer

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Q3 revenue budget monitoring report
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Q3 revenue budget report, including a £10k overspend in property. New staff will focus on bringing council properties up to standard.
key_data_points: New joiner and maintenance operative hired
Focus on improving council properties before new leases

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously approved the minutes from the previous meeting.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Cabinet Forward Plan
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Cabinet Forward Plan as part of standard reporting procedures.
key_data_points: Forward Plan noted as part of standard reporting

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes from the 24 November 2025 meeting as a correct record.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Update on recommendations from previous meetings
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously agreed to remove completed recommendations except for two related to member involvement and youth provision.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Draft budget 2026/27 approved with conditions
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee welcomed the draft budget, including a 1.90% council tax increase, and offered to assist with addressing a £4m deficit in 2027/28.

motions.text: The Committee welcomes comprehensive draft budget report and the proposals to extend the council tax exemption to carers;

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Revenue budget monitoring report Q3 2025/26
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee congratulated the Cabinet on closely achieving the budget and requested further details on income generation strategies.

recent_decisions.title: Minutes of previous meeting approved with correction
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes of the November 2025 meeting were approved unanimously, with a correction noting the pond is in Hutton, not Longton.

motions.text: That the minutes of the last meeting be agreed as a correct record for signing by the Mayor, subject to clarification that the pond was situated in Hutton not Longton.

recent_decisions.title: Cabinet report noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Cabinet report was noted, with discussion on garden waste fee increases and conference centre charges.

motions.text: Report was noted.

recent_decisions.title: Audit and Governance report noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Audit and Governance report was noted without discussion.

motions.text: Report was noted.

recent_decisions.title: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny report noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny report was noted without discussion.

motions.text: Report was noted.

recent_decisions.title: Community and External Scrutiny report noted
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Community and External Scrutiny report was noted without discussion.

motions.text: Report was noted.

recent_decisions.title: By-election result acknowledged
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council noted the election of Councillor Clare Burton-Johnson to the Broad Oak Ward.

motions.text: That Council notes and acknowledges the appointment.

recent_decisions.title: Appointments to outside bodies and committees
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved appointments to Citizens Advice Bureau, Corporate Scrutiny, and Licensing committees.

motions.text: That Council approves the appointment of Councillor Lesley Pritchard to the Board of West Lancashire’s Citizens Advice Bureau.
That Councillor Burton-Johnson be appointed to the Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee and Councillor David Howarth be removed.
That Councillor David Howarth be appointed to the Licensing and Public Safety Committee and Councillor Angela Turner be removed.

recent_decisions.title: Mayor and Deputy Mayor nominations for 2026/2027
recent_decisions.political_summary: Amendment to nominate Michael Green as Deputy Mayor-elect was defeated 19-23.

motions.text: That the vote on the amendment be undertaken.
That Councillor Michael Green be nominated Deputy Mayor-elect for 2026-2027 with a view to becoming Mayor in 2027-2028.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of McDonald's drive-thru restaurant at Riverside Filling Station
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved a McDonald's drive-thru restaurant on Preston New Road, Samlesbury, following the officers' recommendation.

motions.text: The officers’ recommendation to approve the application, subject to the imposition of conditions outlined within the report was proposed by Councillor Phil Smith and seconded by Councillor Keith Martin.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of residential conversion at Rose of Farington
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the conversion of the Rose of Farington pub into 10 residential apartments, following the officers' recommendation.

motions.text: The officers’ recommendation to approve the application, subject to the imposition of conditions outlined within the report was proposed by Councillor Phil Smith and seconded by Councillor Haydn Williams.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of previous meeting minutes
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved the minutes of the previous meeting with 9 votes in favor and 1 abstention.

motions.text: That the minutes of the last meeting be agreed as a correct record for signing by the Chair.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of minutes from previous meeting
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes from the November 2025 Cabinet meeting were unanimously approved as a correct record.

recent_decisions.title: Nomination of Mayor-elect and Deputy Mayor-elect for 2026/27
recent_decisions.political_summary: Councillor Caleb Tomlinson was nominated as Mayor-elect and Councillor Ian Watkinson as Deputy Mayor-elect for 2026/27, with a 6-0 vote.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of Digital Strategy 2025-2028
recent_decisions.political_summary: The refreshed Digital Strategy for 2025-2028 was unanimously approved, focusing on digital transformation for residents, businesses, and staff.
key_data_points: Four key themes: People, Place, Work, Data

recent_decisions.title: Approval of draft 2026/27 budget for consultation
recent_decisions.political_summary: The draft budget for 2026/27 was approved for consultation, including a 1.90% council tax increase and a £400k community fund.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of 3.8% increase in fees and charges for 2026/27
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved a 3.8% increase in core leisure fees and charges, with other fees rising by up to £2, following a unanimous vote.
key_data_points: Other fees rise by up to £2

recent_decisions.title: Noting of Q3 revenue budget monitoring report
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet noted the Q3 revenue budget monitoring report, including a £10k overspend in property maintenance. The council will focus on bringing properties up to standard before new leases.
key_data_points: New joiner and maintenance operative hired
Focus on property standards before new leases

recent_decisions.title: Exclusion of press and public for confidential items
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously voted to exclude the press and public from confidential items, citing exempt information under the Local Government Act 1972.
key_data_points: Based on exempt information under Local Government Act 1972

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of previous meetings
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the minutes of the meetings held on 15 September 2025 and 10 November 2025 as accurate records.

motions.text: That the minutes of the meeting, held on Monday 15 September 2025, are agreed as an accurate record for signing by the Chair.
That the minutes of the meeting, held on Monday 10 November 2025, are agreed as an accurate record for signing by the Chair.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted progress on recommendations
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted progress updates on recommendations and agreed to remove completed ones from the tracking document.

motions.text: That the recommendations marked as completed be removed, except for two items requiring further clarity.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted performance monitoring report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the quarterly performance report, highlighting 2 projects off target and concerns about customer satisfaction and staff sickness.
key_data_points: Staff sickness levels higher than similar authorities

motions.text: That the Committee welcomes the comprehensive draft budget report and the proposals to extend the council tax exemption to carers.
That the Committee looks forward to receiving copies of the impact assessment associated with the car parking strategy.
That the Committee offers to assist the Cabinet as proposals to meet the £4m deficit for 2027/28 as they are developed.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted Jubilee Gardens progress
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted that 14 apartments in Jubilee Gardens extra-care facility are now occupied, with full occupancy expected by Summer 2026.

motions.text: That the Committee congratulates the Cabinet and Officers on closely achieving the budget and for the comprehensive report.
That the Committee suggests that the Cultural Strategy and Commercial Strategy makes the most of the Civic Centre Conference facilities as an asset.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted leisure centre refurbishments
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted that leisure centre refurbishments are substantially complete, with positive feedback and financial targets being met.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted participatory democracy project challenges
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted challenges with the participatory democracy project, including higher-than-anticipated costs and potential opportunities through Local Government Reorganisation.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted Digital Panel accessibility
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the council's commitment to ensuring digital exclusion is addressed for the Digital Panel.

recent_decisions.committee: Corporate, Performance and Budget Scrutiny Committee
recent_decisions.title: Noted Penwortham Masterplan progress
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted progress on the Penwortham Masterplan, including procurement for Kingsfold and Middleforth elements, with work expected to start in Summer 2026.
